topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box sat on the dusty attic floor, a cardboard sarcophagus filled with crumpled papers. Liam stared at it, his jaw tight. His mother, bless her oblivious heart, had always been meticulous about her work, but this—this entire accumulation of forgotten homework, test sheets, and doodles from years past—was an affront to his sense of order. He kicked at a stack of geometry proofs, the sound sharp in the silence. "Seriously?" he muttered, the word tasting like ash. He felt a hot flush creep up his neck. It was an overwhelming sense of exasperation, a feeling that tightened his chest until breathing became shallow. He wanted to sweep the whole thing into a dumpster, to erase the evidence of this chaotic hoarding.

[story 2]

I found the ledger tucked inside an old shoebox. It wasn't just assignments; it was every single graded paper from middle school. My hands started to tremble as I flipped through the pages, the ink blurring under my anxious gaze. A low, guttural sound escaped my throat. This wasn't just clutter; it was a deliberate display of some strange, intense fixation. I slammed the box shut, the resulting thud echoing loudly in the hallway. I paced back and forth, my movements jerky and agitated. Every breath felt shallow, constricted by a rising tide of sheer frustration. Why couldn't she just *throw* things away?

[story 3]

The sheer volume of completed essays hit me like a physical wave. Sarah leaned against the doorframe, watching my reaction. I didn't speak; I just gripped the edge of the desk until my knuckles were white. My eyes darted from one piece of paper to the next, a frantic, restless energy vibrating beneath my skin. It was infuriating, this relentless preservation of every single academic effort. I pushed away from the desk with unnecessary force, the chair scraping loudly against the wooden floor. A deep, simmering resentment brewed inside me, making my entire body tense, ready to snap.
